summary: End-to-end testing of Nitro Nexus (FastAPI + React 3D marketplace). Backend: 19/19 pytest cases pass (products, orders, auth, admin CRUD, stats, auth guards). Frontend: after fixing a pointer-events bug on product cards, all critical flows pass (home + 3D hero + product grid, category filter, search, add-to-cart from card & detail page, cart qty inc/dec, checkout success, admin login/protected route/dashboard stats/product CRUD/orders tab/logout).

ui_bugs.issue: Decorative gradient overlay pseudo-element covered the entire card (position:absolute; inset:0) without pointer-events:none, intercepting all clicks on Add-to-cart button and product Link. This blocked add-to-cart, product detail navigation, cart page and checkout flows. Fixed by adding pointer-events:none to .tracing-border::after in /app/frontend/src/index.css.

action_items: The pointer-events fix is a functional CSS bug fix — please confirm the change in src/index.css is acceptable. No further action needed to unblock e2e flows.
Consider adding an integration/e2e Playwright test in the repo to guard against regressions on product card interactions.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: JWT is signed with a static secret from .env and access token TTL is 7 days — reasonable for MVP but consider adding a refresh-token flow / shorter expiry for production.
server.py: seed_admin() correctly re-hashes when the plaintext password changes (idempotent).

server.py: /api/orders does not enforce stock decrement or verify items[].price against products collection — a client could submit any total. Acceptable for the mock-order MVP but flag for follow-up.
server.py: uses deprecated @app.on_event(startup/shutdown) — will emit FastAPI deprecation warnings; migrate to lifespan handlers.
index.css: .tracing-border::after previously blocked pointer events (now fixed).
AuthContext.js likely calls /api/auth/me on mount even when there is no token, producing a benign 401 in the browser console; consider guarding the call when nn_token is absent.
Product images use unsplash URLs — acceptable placeholders but not truly domain-relevant (Discord Nitro image is generic phone screen).
test_credentials: admin@nitronexus.gg / Admin@12345 (JWT stored in localStorage as nn_token)
seed_data_creation: None — used product/admin seed data auto-created by backend startup. Test data created during runs is prefixed TEST_ (products cleaned up in test_product_crud; orders like TEST_Buyer remain in DB but are harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). All flows verified against the public preview URL. If the tracing-border CSS gets refactored, ensure pointer-events:none stays on the ::after overlay to keep product-card interactions clickable.
rca of the issue: Root cause: .tracing-border::after in /app/frontend/src/index.css was declared with position:absolute; inset:0 to render an animated gold gradient border but lacked pointer-events:none. The pseudo-element sits above the card content in stacking order and captured all mouse events, so clicks on the Add-to-cart button and the <Link> wrapping the product image/title never reached them. Reproduction: navigate to home, scroll to catalog, attempt to click any Add button — Playwright reports '<div class="tracing-border ..."> intercepts pointer events'. Mitigation: added pointer-events:none to the ::after pseudo-element. After the fix, all cart/detail/checkout Playwright flows pass.
